cards.grammarNote: 있어요 expresses both existence (there is) and possession (I have). 직업이 있어요 = I have a job. 시간이 있어요 = I have time.
없어요 = I don't have / there isn't. Never use 안 with 없다 — 없어요 is already negative. 직업이 없어요 = I don't have a job.
직업 = job. 이 = subject marker (consonant-ending noun). 있어요 = have/exist. 직업이 있어요 = I have a job.
경험 = experience. 경험이 있어요 = I have experience. Used commonly in job interviews and professional contexts.
시간 = time. 이 = subject marker. 있어요 with rising intonation = question. 시간이 있어요? = Do you have time?

cards.front: I don't have a driver's license.
cards.grammarNote: 운전면허 = driver's license. 가 = subject marker (vowel-ending noun). 없어요 = I don't have.
재능 = talent/gift. 재능이 있어요 = I have talent. 재능이 없어요 = I have no talent. Common in hobby and skill contexts.
취미 = hobby. 가 = subject marker (vowel ending). 취미가 있어요 = I have a hobby / hobbies. 취미가 없어요 = I don't have any hobbies.
이력서 = résumé/CV. 있어요? = do you have? No particle needed in informal spoken Korean for a quick question.
휴가 = vacation/leave. 가 = subject marker. 휴가가 있어요 = I have vacation time. 휴가가 없어요 = I have no vacation.
질문 = question. 있어요 = have/exist. 질문 있어요? = Any questions? Commonly asked by teachers or presenters.
